questions.question: Which omega-3 fatty acid has the strongest evidence for antidepressant effects when used as supplementation?
questions.explanation: EPA-predominant supplementation shows the strongest antidepressant effect. The mechanism involves reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-6, TNF-alpha), enhancing serotonin receptor sensitivity, and improving membrane fluidity in the prefrontal cortex.

questions.question: What happens when the IDO enzyme is activated by chronic inflammation?

options.text: It increases serotonin production in the brain
It converts dopamine into norepinephrine more efficiently
It diverts tryptophan away from serotonin production into the neurotoxic kynurenine pathway

questions.explanation: Chronic inflammation activates the IDO enzyme, which shunts tryptophan down the kynurenine pathway instead of converting it to serotonin. This produces neurotoxic metabolites while simultaneously depleting serotonin precursors — a dual insult that explains why some individuals do not respond fully to SSRIs.

questions.question: Why is L-methylfolate (5-MTHF) preferred over standard folic acid for depression treatment?

options.text: It is significantly cheaper than folic acid supplements
It bypasses the MTHFR enzyme entirely, making it effective regardless of genetic polymorphism status
It only needs to be taken once per month
It directly increases dopamine without affecting serotonin

questions.explanation: L-methylfolate bypasses the MTHFR enzyme entirely, making it effective regardless of genetic polymorphism status. Up to 40% of some populations carry MTHFR polymorphisms that impair folate metabolism, and approximately 30% of individuals with treatment-resistant depression have suboptimal folate status.

questions.question: According to the SMILES trial, what percentage of participants with moderate-to-severe depression achieved remission through Mediterranean-style dietary counseling?
questions.explanation: The SMILES trial demonstrated that dietary counseling focused on Mediterranean-style eating produced remission in 32% of participants with moderate-to-severe depression — more than triple the rate in the social support control group (8%).

questions.question: Why should SAMe (S-Adenosylmethionine) NOT be combined with serotonergic medications without medical supervision?

options.text: SAMe neutralizes the effects of all antidepressants
SAMe causes severe liver damage when combined with any medication
The combination carries a risk of serotonin syndrome, a potentially life-threatening condition
SAMe is only effective when taken without any other supplements

questions.explanation: SAMe increases serotonin, norepinephrine, and dopamine levels. Combining it with serotonergic medications (SSRIs, SNRIs, MAOIs) can lead to excessive serotonergic activity and serotonin syndrome — a potentially fatal medical emergency.

title: Nutrition for Major Depressive Disorder - Quiz
